levels.body: The proverb ‘to be worth one's salt' is defined as someone being "efficient or capable" ("salt, *n*.1." 2.g.).
worksCited: "salt, *n*.1." *OED Online*, Oxford University Press, December 2021, www.oed.com/view/Entry/170146. Accessed 6 January 2022.

levels.body: The proverb "to be worth one's salt" has its origins in the Roman conquest of Britain. Roman soldiers are believed to have been partly paid in salt, which was a valuable and expensive commodity (cf. "Roman Times").
Toby is proudly convinced to do a good a job and that he is worth the money, he is being paid.
